preamble: Epenthetic [b] insertion, often called excrescence, typically occurs to bridge the transition between a nasal and a liquid sound, easing the flow of air from a closed to an open state.

examples.note: Excrescence: [b] inserted between bilabial nasal [m] and lateral [l].
Via Old French 'humble'; [b] inserted between [m] and [l].
Via Old French 'nombre'; [b] inserted between [m] and [r].

sources: Lass, R. (1984). Phonology: An Introduction to Basic Concepts. Cambridge University Press.
